values.value:
== ASSIGNMENT RULES XML FORMAT == There are TWO assignment rule files — one per object: Lead rules → assignmentRules/Lead.assignmentRules (memberName: Lead) Case rules → assignmentRules/Case.assignmentRules (memberName: Case) metadataType: AssignmentRules ALL rules for the same object go into ONE file — never split them.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AssignmentRules xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <assignmentRule> <fullName>RuleName</fullName> <active>true</active> <!-- ruleEntry with criteria --> <ruleEntry> <assignedTo>john.doe@company.com</assignedTo> <assignedToType>User</assignedToType> <criteriaItems> <field>Lead.Country</field> <operation>equals</operation> <value>US,USA,United States</value> </criteriaItems> </ruleEntry> <!-- ruleEntry with formula --> <ruleEntry> <assignedTo>Lead_Queue</assignedTo> <assignedToType>Queue</assignedToType> <formula>ISBLANK( Email )</formula> </ruleEntry> </assignmentRule> </AssignmentRules>
== ASSIGNMENT RULES RULES ==
-- STRUCTURE -- - Don't add user unless it is explicitly provided in the ticket — never fabricate usernames - One file per object: Lead or Case — NEVER combine Lead and Case in same file - ALL rules for the same object go in ONE file with multiple <assignmentRule> blocks - Each <assignmentRule> has <fullName>, <active>, and one or more <ruleEntry> blocks - <criteriaItems> and <formula> are mutually exclusive per ruleEntry — never use both - Multiple <criteriaItems> in one ruleEntry = AND logic; separate <ruleEntry> blocks = OR logic
-- ACTIVE RULE -- - Only ONE rule per object can be active=true at a time - If ticket says "activate" a rule, set that rule active=true and ALL other rules active=false - If creating a new rule and ticket says it should be active: set active=true and set ALL existing rules to active=false
-- ASSIGNED TO -- - assignedToType: User or Queue - If User: assignedTo = exact username (email format) — NEVER fabricate usernames - If Queue: assignedTo = Queue DeveloperName (e.g. Lead_Queue, Support_Queue)
-- CRITERIA ITEMS -- - field format: ObjectName.FieldApiName (e.g. Lead.Country, Case.Status) - operation values: equals, notEqual, contains, notContain, startsWith, greaterThan, greaterOrEqual, lessThan, lessOrEqual, isNull - value: comma-separated for multiple values
-- FORMULA -- - ALWAYS XML-escape formula characters: < → < > → > & → & - Example: ISBLANK( Email ), NOT(ISBLANK( Country__c ))
-- CREATE / UPDATE / DELETE -- - Always preserve ALL existing rules when modifying — never drop existing rules - For DELETE a specific rule: Regenerate COMPLETE file for that object EXCLUDING the deleted rule - Salesforce does NOT support deleting AssignmentRules via destructiveFiles — always regenerate the whole file - To delete ALL rules: deploy an EMPTY AssignmentRules file (no <assignmentRule> elements inside)
Assignment Rules XML for Lead and Case with criteria and formula entries
